title: Completely changed how I cook
text: I resisted buying an Instant Pot for years. Now I use it 4-5 times a week. Dried beans from scratch in 35 minutes. Whole chicken in 25 minutes. Brown rice in 22 minutes. It has genuinely changed how I approach weeknight cooking. The learning curve takes about a week but after that it becomes second nature.

title: Lid seal needs attention
text: The cooker itself is excellent but the silicone sealing ring absorbs food odors aggressively. After making a curry the smell was in every subsequent dish for two weeks. I bought a second ring just for savory dishes and a third for sweet. This is a well-known issue but Instant Pot should address it or include two rings in the box.

title: Perfect for meal prep
text: I do Sunday meal prep for the whole week and this has cut my time in half. Hard boiled eggs in 5 minutes, pulled chicken in 20 minutes, big batch of steel cut oats in 4 minutes. The keep-warm function is genuinely useful. Build quality feels solid after 18 months of heavy use.

title: Great but the interface is confusing
text: The cooking results are excellent — I am giving 4 stars. The interface has 13 preset buttons and none of them are labeled with the time or temperature they use. You have to look it up in the manual every time until you memorize it. A simple screen showing the active settings would fix this.

title: Replaced 6 appliances
text: I had a rice cooker, slow cooker, steamer, and sauté pan all taking up counter space. This replaces all of them. The rice is genuinely excellent — better than my dedicated rice cooker. The yogurt function works. The only thing I do not use is the sterilize function but that is not a complaint.

title: Steam release scared me at first
text: The pressure release valve makes a loud noise the first time you hear it. I nearly dropped it. Once you know what to expect it is completely fine. The manual could warn new users about this. Beyond that, this is an excellent product — I use it at least three times a week.

title: Built-in saute is genuinely useful
text: The ability to sauté onions and brown meat in the same pot before pressure cooking means one less pan to wash. That single feature alone justifies the purchase for me. Food tastes more developed because you can build flavor before sealing.
